California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
March 31, 1987 
Ear 
Wilson, City Manager 
Ci y of Gustine 
P. O. Box 306 
Gust ine, CA 
95322 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-070 
Dear Mr. Wilson: 
This is in response to your request for advice with 
regard to the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of 
the Political Reform Act.]/ 
ESTION 
When a city contracts with a law firm to provide services 
as city attorney, is the firm required to file a statement of 
economic interests, or is the individual attorney required to 
file the statement? 
CONCLUSION 
The individual attorney who provides services to the 
city must file the statement of economic interests. 
that: 
ANALYSIS 
In Section 81002, the Act states as one of its purposes 
Assets and income of public officials which may 
be materially affected by their official actions 
should be disclosed and in appropriate circumstances

be avoided. 
or 
hat conflicts of 
Se 
n 81002 (c) . 
Th 
Act defines "public offic al" as "every member, 
officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government 
ag(~ncy" (Section 82048), and specifj s the public officials, 
including 
ity attorneys, who are required to file statements 
of econom c interests (Form 721). 
(Sections 87200-87208.) 
Based on the foregoing, it 1S 
lear that the conflict of 
interest disclosure provisions of the Act apply t.o individuals 
who are employed by or provide services to state and local govern-
ment agencies, and not to business entities which contract to 
provide services to such agencies. 
Ther fore, when a law firm 
is hired to provide services as ci 
attorney, the individual 
attorney WllO provides the services must file periodic statements 
of economic interests (Form 721) pursuant to Section 87200. 
If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate 
to contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
Genera] Counsel 
By

Political Reform Consultant 


Earl Wilson

February 27, 1987 
Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, Ca. 
95804-0807 
This is in regard to the amendment to the Political Reform Act 
(1985 Stats. Chapter 611) which requires City Attorneys to file a 
Form 721. 
If the City Attorney services are contracted rather 
than being performed by an employee, is the contract individual 
or firm required to make a filing? 
Your response as soon as possible is appreciated. 
If you need 
any further details regarding our City Attorney services to answer 
this request, please contact me. 
Earl Wilson 
City Manager 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Earl Wilson 
City Manager 
City of Gustine 
March 3, 1987 
P.O. Box 306 
~. 
Irvine, CA 
95322 
Re: 
87-070 
Dear Mr. Wilson: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on March 2, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days. 
You also should be aware that your 
letter and our response are public records which may be 
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours,

sections.conclusion:
The individual attorney who provides services to the 
city must file the statement of economic interests.
